notification_text: Elara looks at you differently today. She doesn't say why. But her hand hovers near her herb pouch — the one with the sedatives — whenever you're close. Someone told her you've been seen with Fenrick.
Commander Voss meets your eyes across the village square. Her expression is unreadable. Then she turns away. Word travels fast in a dying village.

You notice Greta's inn is busier than ever. Not with drinkers — with desperate people buying the last of her food stores at triple prices. She catches your eye and looks away. Even kindness has its limits when survival is at stake.

Old Maren looks frailer today. The weight of what she knows — her grandson, her granddaughter, the pact that ate her son — is becoming physical. The earth feels heavier to her now, she says. As if the roots are already reaching for her.
The compass needle swings hard toward the crypt entrance. It vibrates in your hand. Silas's compass wants you to go DOWN. Into the dark. Into the place where the dead keep their secrets.

Fenrick catches your eye across the village square. His smirk has an edge to it now. He's heard you've been asking questions about the water. Questions that might lead back to the Guild's... other activities.
Commander Voss nods at you across the camp — but the nod is curt. Professional. She's heard you've been seen in back alleys with Fenrick. Mercenaries survive by knowing who their allies really serve.
Greta catches you at the inn door. Her voice is flat. 'Elara's been lying in her cottage for days. Beaten. Alone. And you — you've been busy, I suppose.' She doesn't wait for an answer.

You walk through the village square and every eye turns away. Not in fear — in contempt. The Guild wants you dead. The Guard wants you gone. The Church has condemned you. The Lord won't speak your name. You have made enemies of everyone. In a dying village, that is the loneliest kind of death.
The NPC gives you a look that says: 'Again?' There's a polite patience in their voice that wasn't there before. Even friendly people have limits.
